title: CLAUDE.md
text:
Project
Robo AI Digest — an automated AI news site at https://roboaidigest.com. Quarto static site that publishes 1–3 daily English-language briefings on AI/ML developments. Hosted on Netlify; deploys read pre-rendered HTML from the committed _site/ directory (Netlify itself does not run Quarto — see “Deployment” below).

title: CLAUDE.md
text:
Common commands
quarto render                       # build full site into _site/
quarto preview                      # local dev server with hot reload
quarto render posts/2026-05-06-eu-ai-act-dma-review/  # render a single post
bash scripts/generate-images.sh     # (re)generate image: URLs in posts (Pollinations.ai)
bash scripts/add_nofollow.sh        # apply rel="nofollow" to external markdown links
There is no test suite, linter, or package manifest — this is a content site, not an application.

title: CLAUDE.md
text:
Publishing flow (the big picture)

Scout — daily cron jobs (08:00, 08:45, 10:15) trigger an agent that reads data/sources.json (RSS feeds, blogs, arXiv) and picks stories. R/automate_scout.R is a stub describing the agent contract; the actual scouting logic is executed by the orchestrating agent, not by R code.
Deduplicate — before writing, the agent must check data/published_topics.json and skip topics already covered. After publishing, it appends new entries (term + slug + date). This file is the single source of truth for “what have we already covered” and must be kept in sync.
Write — create posts/YYYY-MM-DD-slug/index.qmd with the frontmatter conventions below.
Image — scripts/generate-images.sh rewrites the image: field in each post to a https://pollinations.ai/p/<title>?... URL. Cron pipelines run this after writing posts (a past bug: posts went out without thumbnails because this step was missing).
Render & commit — quarto render, then commit both the post directory and the regenerated files under _site/. Netlify serves whatever is in _site/; skipping this step means the post won’t appear live.

title: CLAUDE.md
text:
Post frontmatter conventions
---
title: "..."
date: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM"          # always include the time slot (08:00 / 08:45 / 10:15)
author: "AI News Editorial"
categories:
  - Primary
  - Secondary
summary: "..."                     # 1-sentence dek shown on listings
image: "https://pollinations.ai/p/..."   # set by generate-images.sh
---
CATEGORIES.md defines the canonical 7-category taxonomy (LLMs & Models, Industry News, Agents & Automation, Research & Innovation, AI Infrastructure, AI Security & Policy, AI Tools & Frameworks) with a “two categories per post” rule. Older posts predate this and use ad-hoc categories — don’t retrofit them unless asked.

title: CLAUDE.md
text:
External links: always nofollow
filters/nofollow.lua is registered in _quarto.yml and adds rel="nofollow" to every http(s):// link at render time. You generally don’t need to add {rel="nofollow"} manually in markdown — the Lua filter handles it. The standalone scripts/add_nofollow.sh exists for retrofitting older posts and is rarely needed for new content.

title: CLAUDE.md
text:
Deployment
netlify.toml has only publish = "_site" and no build command. Netlify does not run Quarto. The _site/ directory is committed to git and served as-is. Consequence: any change to a post, theme file, or _quarto.yml requires a local quarto render and a commit of the regenerated _site/ for it to ship. (See .PROJECT_LOG.md 2026-02-10 entry — this was a deliberate fix after Netlify was skipping Quarto builds.)

title: CLAUDE.md
section: Things that look weird but are intentional
text:
Things that look weird but are intentional

_site/ is checked into git (see Deployment).
.PROJECT_LOG.md is dotfile-prefixed specifically so Quarto doesn’t pick it up into the sitemap (PROJECT_LOG.md and _site/PROJECT_LOG.html are also gitignored).
_quarto.yml sets website.title: "" so the article title appears first in the <title> tag for SEO.
header-includes in _quarto.yml wires up Google Consent Mode v2 → Klaro cookie banner → GTM (GTM-WR6KBLTV) in that exact order. Reordering breaks consent gating.
Project log and some shell scripts contain Polish comments — this is a solo project, not a localization issue.

title: CLAUDE.md
section: Reference files when working in this repo
text:
Reference files when working in this repo

CATEGORIES.md — taxonomy rules for new posts.
data/sources.json — feed list the scout agent reads from.
data/published_topics.json — dedup ledger; update when publishing.
.PROJECT_LOG.md — chronological log of incidents, fixes, and infra changes; useful background for “why is X like this”.

title: Samsung Reportedly in Talks for €1B Stake in Mistral at €20B Valuation
text:
Samsung is in advanced talks to invest as much as €1 billion in French AI laboratory Mistral, according to reports from the Financial Times. The investment would be part of a broader funding round valuing the Paris-based company at approximately €20 billion—nearly doubling the €11.7 billion valuation that semiconductor equipment maker ASML anchored earlier this year.
The potential stake represents one of the largest investments by a major tech conglomerate in a European AI startup and signals intensifying competition for access to leading artificial intelligence research. Mistral, founded by former Google DeepMind and Meta researchers, has quickly established itself as Europe’s primary contender in the global AI race through its series of open-weight and proprietary models.
Samsung’s interest in Mistral extends beyond financial returns. The Korean electronics giant has been aggressively expanding its AI infrastructure capabilities, from chip manufacturing to consumer devices. Partnering with Mistral would provide Samsung with cutting-edge AI technology potentially integrated into its smartphones, appliances, and enterprise services.
The investment would build on Samsung’s existing venture capital relationship with Mistral, through which the company’s venture arm has previously backed the French startup. The new commitment would substantially increase that footprint and mark one of the largest foreign investments in a European AI company to date.
If completed, the deal would position Mistral among Europe’s most valuable private AI companies, alongside Anthropic and OpenAI in valuation terms. The investment would also deepen the strategic ties between French AI ambition and Korean technology manufacturing—a combination that could reshape competitive dynamics in the global AI landscape.
The news comes amid heightened M&A activity in the AI sector, with major corporations seeking strategic stakes in promising labs. Mistral has attracted interest from multiple suitors following its rapid rise from startup to European AI champion in under three years.

title: Chinese Open-Weight Models Now Power 60% of US OpenRouter Traffic
text:
US enterprises have quietly become reliant on Chinese artificial intelligence models, with open-weight offerings from Alibaba’s Qwen, DeepSeek, and Moonshot’s Kimi now accounting for roughly 60% of token usage on model marketplace OpenRouter. The scale of adoption complicates any US move to restrict Chinese frontier models—cutting them off would disrupt significant enterprise workloads already routed through these open-weight endpoints.
The finding, reported by Bloomberg, represents a dramatic shift in just months. OpenRouter, which provides a unified API for accessing dozens of language models, has become a critical infrastructure layer for developers seeking to mix and match models based on cost and capability. The dominance of Chinese models reflects their strong performance on coding and reasoning tasks combined with aggressive pricing through API access.
Qwen models have proven particularly popular for enterprise applications requiring strong multilingual capabilities, while DeepSeek’s offerings have gained traction among developers seeking high performance at lower costs. Kimi K3, released earlier this month, has quickly become a favorite for its analytical capabilities despite higher operational costs compared to alternatives.
The adoption pattern poses a challenge for Washington policymakers exploring restrictions on Chinese AI technologies. Many US companies have already built workflows, fine-tuning pipelines, and production systems around these Chinese models. Restricting access would require not just regulatory action but significant engineering effort to migrate to alternatives—a process that could take months and incur substantial costs.
Industry analysts note that the open-weight nature of these models provides some workaround potential even under strict sanctions, as the model weights can theoretically be downloaded and self-hosted. However, this would eliminate the convenience and scalability of API-based access through platforms like OpenRouter.
The development also highlights the success of Chinese AI labs in producing competitive open-weight alternatives to Western frontier models. What began as an effort to catch up has evolved into a situation where Chinese models now lead in certain market segments—a dynamic that US policy has not yet fully addressed.

title: Kategorie dla Robo AI Digest
text:
Oficjalna lista (7 kategorii):

LLMs & Models - nowe modele (GPT, Claude, Gemini, Qwen, etc.)
Industry News - firmy, funding, akwizycje, partnerstwa
Agents & Automation - agenci, workflow, automatyzacja
Research & Innovation - badania, benchmarki, przełomy
AI Infrastructure - hardware, chipy, GPU, inference
AI Security & Policy - wojsko, regulacje, etyka, prawo
AI Tools & Frameworks - open source, biblioteki, narzędzia

title: About Robo AI Digest
text:
The Mission: Signal Over Noise
In a world drowning in AI hype and daily announcements, Robo AI Digest was born from a simple necessity: the need for clarity. Our mission is to filter the global flow of information and deliver a precise, reliable, and accessible briefing on what truly matters in the world of Artificial Intelligence.

title: About Robo AI Digest
text:
What We Cover


Intelligence & LLMs — shifts in Large Language Models and Foundation models.
Workplace Automation — tools and workflows that actually increase productivity.
Industry Breakthroughs — advancements that move the needle in business and research.
Policy & Ethics — regulations, safety frameworks, and societal impact.

title: Terms of Service
text:
Description of Service
Robo AI Digest provides curated summaries of artificial intelligence news, research updates, and industry developments. Our content consists of:

Daily AI news summaries
Analysis of AI industry trends
Coverage of research breakthroughs
Tool and model updates

title: Terms of Service
text:
Fair Use and Content Policy

Content Sources
We gather information from publicly available news sources, research papers, company announcements, and industry reports. All content is summarized and rewritten to create original commentary.


Fair Use Doctrine
Our summaries and analysis fall under fair use doctrine because they: - Transform original content through summarization and analysis - Serve educational and informational purposes - Do not substitute for original source material - Include proper attribution when applicable


Content Ownership

Original summaries and analysis are owned by Robo AI Digest
Source materials remain the property of their respective owners
We respect intellectual property rights and copyright laws

title: Terms of Service
text:
User Responsibilities

Acceptable Use
You may: - Read and share our content for personal, non-commercial use - Quote brief excerpts with proper attribution - Link to our articles


Prohibited Activities
You may not: - Republish full articles without permission - Use automated scrapers to harvest our content - Modify or redistribute our content commercially - Misrepresent our content as your own
